name: Wynonie Harris
abstract: Wynonie Harris (August 24, 1915 – June 14, 1969) was an American blues shouter best remembered as a singer of upbeat songs, featuring humorous, often ribald lyrics. He had fifteen Top 10 hits between 1946 and 1952.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...I don't know if you remember a blues singer named Wynonie Harris?

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: We backed Wynonie Harris, the Blues singer, on his first big hit, "Around The Clock." ... And that was his first big hit, and I had a solo on that record.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: I had did the things for Wynonie Harris... I did a lot of things personally for Glover like 'All She Wanna Do Is Rock with Wynonie Harris'... I did 'All She Wanna Do I Rock,' which was a smash hit for Wynonie Harris.

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth Brown
about_quotes.evidence: other artists like myself, — Jackie Wilson, The Drifters, The Clovers, The Flamingos, Chuck Willis, Roy Brown, Charles Brown, Wynonie Harris, to name only a few — was referred to as race music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth Brown interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Wynonie Harris in the context of a recording session, but the INTERVIEWEE only responds 'Right' without confirming any personal interaction or relationship with Harris.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: And in fact I was thinking about Jo Jones’ cousin was Wynonie Harris... He called me “Miss Box.” Because he called the piano a box. He said “That’s Miss Box. Miss Box is here.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998
